description: A backfill double-writes because tasks append rather than overwrite their date partition.
body: Failure mode: a backfill reruns historical dates against tasks that append rows keyed on insertion time, so each reprocessed date duplicates data already present and downstream aggregates overcount. Detection signals: row counts for backfilled dates higher than the original scheduled runs, and totals that climb each time a backfill is repeated. Do not re-run the backfill to 'refresh' the numbers. Safer fallback: make each task overwrite or merge its own execution-date partition idempotently, truncate the duplicated partitions, re-run the backfill within bounded concurrency, and reconcile a sample against the original scheduled output before trusting it.
